description: To hold hope for a particular outcome, to want something to come to pass. 'Pula' names the space between present and desired future—the heart's orientation toward what might be, held gently without grasping.
sound_symbolism: The word is brief like a wish sent forth. 'Pu' pushes gently with labial release, desire emerging. Then 'la' flows with liquid ease, the wish traveling toward its hoped-for destination.
grammatical_notes: As verb: 'mi lo wela pula' (I wish for good). As noun: 'lo wela pula' (a good wish). Can take clause complements: 'mi pula [clause]' (I wish that...). Distinct from wanting (which implies lack)—pula holds hope lightly.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Wishing practiced skillfully avoids attachment—we can hope for outcomes while accepting whatever arises.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Expressing wishes shares inner hopes without demanding—it invites rather than commands.
